notes: level 14 column pour today. got 23 columns to do. concrete booked from 6am. lets gooo
first agitator just arrived. 8 cubic metres. starting with the corner columns on grid A1 through A4
pump operator is setting up the line boom on level 14. hose running from the ground floor pump
formwork was checked yesterday but doing a final walk now. checking all the kickers and brace
bracing* autocorrect lol
found one column form on C3 that wasnt fully tied at the base. fixed it. would have leaked everywhere
reo has all been inspected and tagged. engineer was here yesterday. all good on the steel
pour started 6:20. first column done in about 15 mins. using a vibrator to consolidate properly
we're getting into a rhythm now. averaging about 12-15 mins per column with the vibe and topping off
second truck is here, first ones nearly empty. good timing
temp is about 8 degrees this morning. cold. the concrete mix has been adjusted for cold weather curing, the batch plant added accelerator
safety officer doing his rounds. checked that everyone has hard hats, hi vis, steel caps. all good except one of the concreters had cracked safety glasses. gave him a new pair
were up to column 10 now. going well. no issues with the concrete, good slump, flowing nicely into the forms
the slump test on the second truck was 80mm which is within spec. 80 plus or minus 20 so were sweet
oh wait I think the spec says 100 plus minus 20. let me check... yeah its 100 plus minus 20 so 80 is the minimum. just scraped in
actually let me double check that with the engineer. dont want to be borderline on this
engineer confirmed 80 is acceptable for columns. we're good
12 columns done by 9:30. smoko
the pump blocked up after smoko. took about 20 mins to clear it. was a rock or something in the line
back pouring now. lost a bit of time but should still finish today no worries
we're getting some bleed water on the columns we poured first. thats normal for this mix in cold weather. will re-vibrate and top off later
truck 4 just arrived. ordering truck 5 for after lunch
some of the formwork bolts on D2 column look a bit rusty. I mean the form ties not the bolts. theyre functional still but should probably replace them for next pour
20 columns done. 3 to go. last truck should cover it
and done! all 23 columns poured by 2:15pm. finishing up the tops now, troweling smooth
gonna set up the curing compound spray once the surface has firmed up a bit. probably in an hour or so
cylinders taken, 6 test cylinders from todays pour. labeled and in the curing box. 7 day and 28 day breaks
stripping the columns can happen thursday arvo, engineer wants 48 hours minimum cure before we strip. that takes us to thursday 2pm
good day, no safety issues, no concrete rejects, all columns done. onto the level 14 slab formwork tomorrow
